az-204.title: AZ-204 Developing Solutions for Microsoft Azure Practice Exam

topics.name: Develop Azure compute solutions
modules: Implement Azure App Service Web Apps

topics.name: Develop for Azure storage
modules: Develop solutions that use Azure Cosmos DB
Develop solutions that use Azure Blob Storage

topics.name: Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize Azure solutions
modules: Integrate caching and content delivery within solutions
Instrument solutions to support monitoring and logging

topics.name: Connect to and consume Azure services and third-party services

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?
questions.explanation: Queue trigger automatically scales and processes messages from Storage queues.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.2: Runs on schedule.
why_others_wrong.3: Runs when a blob is created or updated.

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.1: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.2: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.
why_others_wrong.3: RU/s is configurable and scalable.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Embed the secret in source code
Pass the secret via query string
Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Never store secrets in code.
why_others_wrong.1: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.
why_others_wrong.2: Insecure and logged in many places.

questions.question: You need ordered, transactional messaging with competing consumers. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions, transactions, and advanced messaging patterns.

reasoning.correct: Designed for enterprise messaging semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Big data streaming ingestion, not work queues.
why_others_wrong.1: Pub/sub for discrete events, not transactional messaging.
why_others_wrong.3: Simple queueing without transactions/sessions.

questions.question: What feature helps you trace a request across microservices hosted on App Service and Functions?
questions.explanation: Distributed tracing correlates telemetry across services.

reasoning.correct: Enables end-to-end diagnostics and performance analysis.

why_others_wrong.0: Dashboards; not automatic correlation.
why_others_wrong.1: Platform incidents; not app traces.
why_others_wrong.2: Ping tests; not end-to-end tracing.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.1: Runs when a blob is created or updated.
why_others_wrong.2: Runs on schedule.

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.2: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.3: RU/s is configurable and scalable.
why_others_wrong.4: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Embed the secret in source code
Pass the secret via query string
Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Never store secrets in code.
why_others_wrong.1: Insecure and logged in many places.
why_others_wrong.2: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.

questions.question: You need ordered, transactional messaging with competing consumers. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions, transactions, and advanced messaging patterns.

reasoning.correct: Designed for enterprise messaging semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Simple queueing without transactions/sessions.
why_others_wrong.2: Big data streaming ingestion, not work queues.
why_others_wrong.3: Pub/sub for discrete events, not transactional messaging.

questions.question: What feature helps you trace a request across microservices hosted on App Service and Functions?
questions.explanation: Distributed tracing correlates telemetry across services.

reasoning.correct: Enables end-to-end diagnostics and performance analysis.

why_others_wrong.1: Platform incidents; not app traces.
why_others_wrong.2: Ping tests; not end-to-end tracing.
why_others_wrong.3: Dashboards; not automatic correlation.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.1: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.2: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.
why_others_wrong.3: RU/s is configurable and scalable.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Embed the secret in source code
Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
Pass the secret via query string
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.
why_others_wrong.1: Never store secrets in code.
why_others_wrong.3: Insecure and logged in many places.

questions.question: You need ordered, transactional messaging with competing consumers. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions, transactions, and advanced messaging patterns.

reasoning.correct: Designed for enterprise messaging semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Pub/sub for discrete events, not transactional messaging.
why_others_wrong.1: Big data streaming ingestion, not work queues.
why_others_wrong.3: Simple queueing without transactions/sessions.

questions.question: What feature helps you trace a request across microservices hosted on App Service and Functions?
questions.explanation: Distributed tracing correlates telemetry across services.

reasoning.correct: Enables end-to-end diagnostics and performance analysis.

why_others_wrong.0: Platform incidents; not app traces.
why_others_wrong.2: Ping tests; not end-to-end tracing.
why_others_wrong.3: Dashboards; not automatic correlation.

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?
questions.explanation: Queue trigger automatically scales and processes messages from Storage queues.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.1: Runs when a blob is created or updated.
why_others_wrong.3: Runs on schedule.

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.0: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.

why_others_wrong.4: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
Pass the secret via query string
Embed the secret in source code
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.1: Insecure and logged in many places.
why_others_wrong.2: Never store secrets in code.
why_others_wrong.3: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.

reasoning.correct: Designed for enterprise messaging semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Pub/sub for discrete events, not transactional messaging.
why_others_wrong.2: Big data streaming ingestion, not work queues.
why_others_wrong.3: Simple queueing without transactions/sessions.

questions.question: What feature helps you trace a request across microservices hosted on App Service and Functions?
questions.explanation: Distributed tracing correlates telemetry across services.

reasoning.correct: Enables end-to-end diagnostics and performance analysis.

why_others_wrong.0: Ping tests; not end-to-end tracing.
why_others_wrong.2: Dashboards; not automatic correlation.
why_others_wrong.3: Platform incidents; not app traces.

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?
questions.explanation: Queue trigger automatically scales and processes messages from Storage queues.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Runs when a blob is created or updated.
why_others_wrong.1: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.2: Runs on schedule.

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.1: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.2: RU/s is configurable and scalable.
why_others_wrong.4: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Embed the secret in source code
Pass the secret via query string
Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.
why_others_wrong.1: Never store secrets in code.
why_others_wrong.2: Insecure and logged in many places.

Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
Embed the secret in source code
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Insecure and logged in many places.
why_others_wrong.1: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.
why_others_wrong.3: Never store secrets in code.

questions.question: You need ordered, transactional messaging with competing consumers. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions, transactions, and advanced messaging patterns.

reasoning.correct: Designed for enterprise messaging semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Simple queueing without transactions/sessions.
why_others_wrong.2: Pub/sub for discrete events, not transactional messaging.
why_others_wrong.3: Big data streaming ingestion, not work queues.

questions.question: What feature helps you trace a request across microservices hosted on App Service and Functions?
questions.explanation: Distributed tracing correlates telemetry across services.

reasoning.correct: Enables end-to-end diagnostics and performance analysis.

why_others_wrong.1: Dashboards; not automatic correlation.
why_others_wrong.2: Platform incidents; not app traces.
why_others_wrong.3: Ping tests; not end-to-end tracing.

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?
questions.explanation: Queue trigger automatically scales and processes messages from Storage queues.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Runs on schedule.
why_others_wrong.1: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.2: Runs when a blob is created or updated.

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.0: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.
why_others_wrong.1: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.3: RU/s is configurable and scalable.

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?
questions.explanation: Queue trigger automatically scales and processes messages from Storage queues.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.1: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.2: Runs on schedule.

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.0: RU/s is configurable and scalable.
why_others_wrong.3: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.4: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Pass the secret via query string
Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
Embed the secret in source code
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.
why_others_wrong.1: Insecure and logged in many places.
why_others_wrong.3: Never store secrets in code.

questions.question: What feature helps you trace a request across microservices hosted on App Service and Functions?
questions.explanation: Distributed tracing correlates telemetry across services.

reasoning.correct: Enables end-to-end diagnostics and performance analysis.

why_others_wrong.1: Dashboards; not automatic correlation.
why_others_wrong.2: Platform incidents; not app traces.
why_others_wrong.3: Ping tests; not end-to-end tracing.

questions.question: Which trigger is appropriate for processing messages from an Azure Storage queue?
questions.explanation: Queue trigger automatically scales and processes messages from Storage queues.

reasoning.correct: Designed for queue message processing with at-least-once semantics.

why_others_wrong.0: Invoked via HTTP, not queue messages.
why_others_wrong.1: Runs when a blob is created or updated.
why_others_wrong.3: Runs on schedule.

questions.question: For an IoT workload requiring low latency and global distribution, which Cosmos DB capabilities are relevant? (Select two)
questions.explanation: Multi-master enables write-side global distribution; partitioning scales throughput horizontally.

reasoning.correct: Both features enable scalability and low-latency writes across regions.

why_others_wrong.0: Stronger or weaker consistency may be needed; not a capability itself.
why_others_wrong.2: Useful for retention but not for global write scalability.
why_others_wrong.3: RU/s is configurable and scalable.

questions.question: How should an App Service app access secrets from Key Vault without storing credentials?
options: Pass the secret via query string
Embed the secret in source code
Use a system-assigned managed identity and Key Vault access policy/RBAC
questions.explanation: Managed identity allows the app to authenticate to Key Vault using Azure AD.

reasoning.correct: Eliminates secret distribution while enabling least-privileged access.

why_others_wrong.0: Insecure and logged in many places.
why_others_wrong.1: Never store secrets in code.
why_others_wrong.2: Secrets in config are risky and require rotation.

questions.question: You need ordered, transactional messaging with competing consumers. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Service Bus supports sessions, transactions, and advanced messaging patterns.

reasoning.correct: Designed for enterprise messaging semantics.

why_others_wrong.2: Big data streaming ingestion, not work queues.

certificationInfo.title: Microsoft Certified: Azure Developer Associate
certificationInfo.description: This certification validates your expertise in designing, building, testing, and maintaining cloud applications and services on Microsoft Azure.
prerequisites: Knowledge of at least one cloud-supported programming language
Understanding of Azure services and workloads, security, and governance

skills: Create Azure App Service web apps
Develop solutions that use Azure Cosmos DB
Develop solutions that use Azure Blob Storage
Develop solutions that use Azure Files
Develop solutions that use Azure Storage Tables

skillsMeasured.category: Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize Azure solutions (15-20%)
skills: Troubleshoot solutions by using Application Insights
Implement code that handles transient faults

skillsMeasured.category: Connect to and consume Azure services and third-party services (15-20%)
